docs.title: Blender Math Anim Add-on
docs.text:
Welcome to the documentation for Blender Math Anim, an advanced add-on for visualizing and animating math functions, rendering formulas, and drawing directly inside Blender.

This site contains:

  • Installation guide
  • Usage tutorials
    • Function plotting and animation workflows
    • Formula generation and animation workflows
    • Drawing & morphing tools
  • API reference

docs.title: Performance Tips
docs.text:
• Use numexpr for fast expression evaluation
  • Default sample count is optimized for animation
  • Heavy curves: disable live update while adjusting parameters
  • Use GPU subdivision sparingly
  • Try to keep expression complexity low

docs.title: Expression Engine (numexpr)
docs.text: The add-on uses numexpr instead of Python eval:

docs.title: Benefits
docs.text:
• Faster evaluation
  • Prevents arbitrary code execution
  • Vectorized math
  • Safe execution sandbox

docs.title: Supported features
docs.text:
• All NumPy-compatible functions
  • Elementwise operations
  • Logical and comparison operators

docs.title: Live Update System
docs.text:
Live update regenerates the curve automatically when:

  • expression changes
  • slider value changes
  • domain or sample count changes

Implementation details: - On property update, compute curve using numexpr - Update mesh vertices only (no recreation) - Threaded execution planned for future versions

docs.title: Add-on Architecture
docs.text: Main components:

docs.title: Function Engine
docs.text:
• Expression parser → variable detection → parameter detection
  • numexpr evaluator
  • Mesh generator

docs.title: UI Panels
docs.text:
• Function panel
  • Drawing panel
  • Formula panel
  • Morph panel
  • Slider panel

docs.title: Drivers and Animation
docs.text:
• Sliders map to keyframes
  • Mesh updates through handlers

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: This page describes the main properties exposed by the add-on.

docs.title: Function Object (MATH_ANIM_Function)
docs.text:
• expression
  • t_min, t_max
  • samples
  • parameters (auto-detected)
  • live_update

docs.title: Formula Object
docs.text:
• input_text
  • font
  • engine (Typst, OpTeX)
  • writing_speed

docs.title: Drawing Object
docs.text:
• points
  • stroke_width
  • color

docs.title: Morph Object
docs.text:
• source
  • target
  • resolution
  • ease_type
  • factor (0 → 1)

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Blender 3.x or newer
  • Python modules (optional features):
  • typst or OpTeX (formula rendering)
  • TrueType or OpenType fonts for formula rendering
  • Supports macOS, Windows, Linux

docs.title: Installing the Add-on
docs.text:
1. Download the .zip from GitHub Releases
  2. Open Blender → Edit → Preferences → Add-ons
  3. Click Install
  4. Select the ZIP file
  5. Enable: Math Anim

docs.title: Setting Up Formula Rendering (optional)
docs.text:
1. In Preferences → Add-ons → Math Anim:
  2. Set Typst executable (if using Typst)
  3. Set OpTeX path (if using OpTeX)
  4. Choose your fonts (.ttf or .otf)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Blender Math Anim is a Blender add-on designed for math visualization, plotting, animation, and drawing inside Blender.

docs.title: ✨ Features
docs.text:
• Generate and animate mathematical functions:
  • explicit functions
  • parametric curves
  • polar functions
  • ODE functions
  • implicit functions
  • Multiple animation options:
  • writing animation
  • parameter driving
  • morphing between plottings, text, drawings
  • Formula generating using Typst / OpTeX / PDF
  • Freehand drawing layer
  • Real-time updates with safe math engine (numexpr)
  • Sliders for any detected parameters

docs.title: Use cases
docs.text:
• Classroom math demonstrations
  • Data visualization
  • TikTok/YouTube math animation
  • Scientific animations
  • Educational content

docs.title: Usage Overview
docs.text: This section covers the core workflow of the add-on.

docs.title: Main components:
docs.text:
• Function Plotting
  • Formula Generating
  • Free Drawing
  • Morphing System

Each tool appears in the Math Anim tab in the 3D View.

docs.title: Freehand Drawing
docs.text:
1. Add → Math Anim → Drawing
  2. Use mouse or tablet to draw paths.
  3. Supports:
  4. Stroke width
  5. Color
  6. Duration
  7. Convert drawing to mesh for animation.

docs.title: Writing Animation
docs.text: Drawing objects support writing reveal animation.

docs.title: Creating a Function Plot
docs.text:
1. Add → Math Anim → Function
  2. Enter a mathematical expression, e.g.: sin(2pit)
  3. Set domain:
  4. t_min, t_max
  5. Set resolution (samples)
  6. Click Generate

docs.title: Supported Expressions
docs.text:
• sin, cos, tan
  • exp, log
  • sqrt
  • abs
  • All NumPy-like operators via numexpr

docs.title: Parameter Sliders
docs.text:
The add-on auto-detects parameters:

Example: sin(a * t + phi) → creates sliders for a, phi.

docs.title: Writing Animation
docs.text: Creates a start-to-finish drawing effect.

docs.title: Parameter Animation
docs.text: Add keyframes to detected sliders.

docs.title: Updating in Real-Time
docs.text: Enable Live Update to update the mesh as sliders change.

docs.title: Morphing
docs.text: You can morph between: - Functions ↔ Functions - Text ↔ Text - Drawing ↔ Drawing - Function ↔ Drawing (after sampling)

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Select A and B
  2. Click: Create Morph
  3. A morph object appears with:
  4. resolution
  5. easing type
  6. duration
  7. Animate morph value from 0 → 1.

docs.title: Formula Rendering
docs.text:
You can create formula objects rendered using Typst or OpTeX.

  1. Add → Math Anim → Formula
  2. Choose engine:
  3. Typst
  4. OpTeX / LaTeX
  5. Enter input text: $ \int_0^\pi \sin(x),dx = 2 $
  6. Render

docs.title: Animating Formulas
docs.text:
• Writing animation
  • Morphing into other formulas
  • Moving along paths
